name: Kettlebell Gorilla Row
resistance_profile: Two kettlebells between the feet in a hinge; pressing down on the resting bell keeps you braced while the other side rows, holding constant mid-range tension on the back.
cues: Push down hard on the resting bell to stay braced
Row elbow to hip, then control it down
execution_steps: Set two kettlebells on the floor between your feet with a roughly shoulder-width stance.
Hinge at the hips into a flat-back bent-over position and grip both bells.
Brace one arm down against its bell to stay rigid while you row the other bell to the hip, elbow driving back.
Lower that bell under control while continuing to press down on the opposite bell.
Alternate arms each rep, keeping the hips still and the spine neutral throughout.
good_when: You want a self-supported bilateral back builder using two kettlebells.
You want to train grip, back, and hinge stability in one movement.
bad_when: You have a cranky lower back that dislikes loaded hinge holds.
You only have a single kettlebell available.
common_errors: Rounding the lower back in the hinge
Bouncing the hips up to help each pull
Letting the torso rotate toward the working side
progressions: Add load or reps, or pause each rep at the top.
Row both bells simultaneously for a heavier bilateral pull.
regressions: Use lighter bells or raise them on blocks to reduce range and hinge demand.
Work with a taller hip angle to lessen lower-back load.
